name: Natural Park of Serra Calderona — Circular from Bétera
name_original: Parque Natural de la Serra Calderona — Circular desde Bétera
region_intro: Road cycling around Bétera offers diverse terrain, combining agricultural land with natural landscapes. The region is characterized by its proximity to the Sierra Calderona Natural Park, which provides significant elevation gains and challenging climbs. Cyclists can explore well-paved surfaces that traverse varied scenery, from cultivated fields to Mediterranean forests. This area caters to a range of fitness levels, featuring both moderate rides and routes with substantial ascents. Best road cycling routes around Bétera The most popular road cycling route is Natural Park of Serra Calderona — Circular from Bétera, a 38.0 miles (61.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 28 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain within the natural park. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Olocau loop from Horta Vella, a moderate 25.7 miles (41.3 km) path. This route explores the natural landscapes and agricultural areas surrounding Bétera. Local road cyclists also love the Olocau – San Vicente de Llíria loop from Horta Vella, a 39.8 miles (64.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain and natural scenery, often completed in about 6 hours. Road cycling around Bétera is defined by agricultural land, natural landscapes, and the Sierra Calderona Natural Park. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging routes with significant elevation gains. The routes in Bétera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 1,700 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Bétera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Bétera?
A: Bétera is a hub for cyclists, offering a wide network of routes. Our guide features 16 distinct road cycling routes, ranging from moderate to difficult, exploring the diverse terrain of the region.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Bétera?
A: The terrain around Bétera is quite varied, combining picturesque agricultural land with natural landscapes. You'll find well-paved surfaces traversing cultivated fields and Mediterranean forests, especially within the Sierra Calderona Natural Park. Routes can range from relatively flat sections to significant climbs.

Q: Are there challenging climbs for experienced road cyclists in Bétera?
A: Yes, Bétera is known for its challenging climbs, particularly within the Sierra Calderona Natural Park. Notable ascents include the classic climb to Puerto del Oronet, which is 5 km long, and the even steeper ramps of La Frontera. For a demanding experience, consider routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e839532515/natural-park-of-serra-calderona-circular-from-betera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Park of Serra Calderona — Circular from Bétera&lt;/a&gt;, which features over 1,100 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Are there any easier or moderate road cycling routes suitable for less experienced riders?
A: While many routes offer significant climbs, there are also moderate options. The region's agricultural lands provide gentler sections. For a moderate ride exploring natural landscapes and agricultural areas, you might enjoy the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39570176" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Olocau loop from Horta Vella&lt;/a&gt;, which covers about 41 km with less demanding elevation.

Q: What natural features and viewpoints can I discover along the routes?
A: Road cycling routes in Bétera offer stunning natural beauty. The Sierra Calderona Natural Park is a central feature, providing diverse Mediterranean forest landscapes and panoramic views. You can reach the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1054060" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garbí Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; for breathtaking vistas of the Bay of Valencia. Other natural highlights include the Carraixet Ravine, known for its horticultural scenery, and various summits like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1044538" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Tristán&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1054055" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mola de Segart&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical sites or landmarks to see while cycling?
A: Yes, Bétera and its surroundings are rich in history. You might encounter 'catxerulos,' old stone structures that served as shelters, along with traditional farmhouses. In the town center, the imposing &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1054054" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castell de Serra&lt;/a&gt;, built on an Arab watchtower, is a notable landmark. Other historical sites include the remains of Roman thermal baths at Horta Vella and the Iberian archaeological site of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2725439" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Puntal dels Llops&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes around Bétera?
A: Yes, many routes around Bétera are designed as loops, offering convenient starting and ending points. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39549917" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Olocau – San Vicente de Llíria loop from Horta Vella&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32372390" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Mallaes – San Vicente de Llíria loop from Horta Vella&lt;/a&gt;, which explore varied terrain and natural scenery.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Bétera?
A: The most popular months for road cycling in Bétera are April and May, when the weather is typically pleasant. However, the region generally offers good conditions for cycling for much of the year, though summer can be quite warm.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Bétera?
A: The road cycling routes around Bétera are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.8 stars from over 20 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, the challenging climbs within the Sierra Calderona, and the beautiful natural and agricultural landscapes.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling options in the area?
A: For a gentler, family-friendly experience, the Carraixet Ravine provides an excellent route for observing the local horticultural landscape. While specific family-focused road cycling routes are not detailed, the agricultural areas generally offer flatter, less challenging sections suitable for a relaxed ride.

Q: Where can I find parking or access routes via public transport?
A: Bétera is well-situated for access. While specific parking locations for each trailhead are not detailed, the town itself offers parking options. For public transport, Bétera is connected to Valencia, making it accessible for cyclists looking to start their rides from the town center. Many routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32641589" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Olocau – Castell de Benisanó loop from Horta Vella&lt;/a&gt;, begin from areas like Horta Vella, which are easily reachable.
tips: Stretch of ascent to Alt del Pi where you can appreciate beautiful views from one of the highest points of the Serra Calderona National Park. The road is in perfect condition which allows you to enjoy it to the fullest!
Whether ascending from the N-234 or descending from El Garbi, this section is truly amazing. The views along the way are amazing and even more so when you reach the top of Garbi.

Section of ascent to one of the emblematic ports of Valencia and the NP Serra la Calderona. You will be rolling down a zigzag path with dense trees and you will find everything you need to refuel in one of the bars and restaurants along the way. A stretch to enjoy and not miss the views from the port!

waypoints.name: Descent from Garbi, through Segart down to the N234
